en: Sally... What's so awful about it?
It's so awful, there's no way to even begin to explain it.
Honey, I don't object to any of your things.
ko: 자기야, 나는 네 물건에 반대하지 않아.

en: If we had an extra room, you could put all your things in it, including your barstools.
ko: 여분의 방이 있다면, 네 모든 물건을 그곳에 넣을 수 있어, 바 스툴도 포함해서.

en: Wait, wait, wait. You don't like my barstools?
ko: 내 바 스툴이 마음에 안 들어?

en: Harry, come on. Someone has to be on my side.
ko: 해리, 제발. 누군가는 내 편이 되어야 해.

en: I'm on your side. I'm just trying to help you have good taste.
ko: 나는 네 편이야. 네가 좋은 취향을 가지도록 도와주고 싶을 뿐이야.

en: I have good taste. Everybody thinks they have good taste and a sense of humor,
but they couldn't possibly all have good taste.
ko: 하지만 모두가 좋은 취향을 가질 수는 없지.

en: You know, it's funny. We started out like this, Helen and I.
ko: 웃기지, 우리도 이렇게 시작했어, 엘렌과 나.

en: We had blank walls, we hung things, we picked out tiles together.
ko: 우리는 빈 벽이었고, 물건을 걸고, 타일을 함께 고르기도 했어.

en: Then you know what happens? Six years later, you find yourself singing Surrey with a fringe on top in front of Ira!
ko: 그러고 나서 무슨 일이 일어나는지 알아?

en: Do we have to talk about this right now? Yes, I think that right now actually is the perfect time to talk about this
because I want our friends to benefit from the wisdom of my experience.
ko: 왜냐하면 내 경험의 지혜를 친구들이 누리게 하고 싶거든.

en: Right now, everything is great. Everyone is happy, everyone is in love, and that's wonderful.
